questions.question: There are eight members in the family. Bravo and Priya are siblings. Angel is Kajal's granddaughter, Kajal who is Priya's mother-in-law. Ziva is a married woman and is older than Tim. Tim is unmarried and is the son of Sam who is the brother-in-law of Bravo. Smith is the eldest male in the family. Angel is not Ziva's daughter. So, how is Bravo related to Ziva?
questions.explanation: Hence, the correct answer is "Option B”

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘ABROGATE’ is coded as ‘77’. How will ‘ACCLAIM’ be coded in the same language?
questions.explanation: A-1,B-2,R-18,O-15,G-7,A-1,T-20,E-5, total no of alphabets=8 Total = 1+2+18+15+7+1+20+5+8=77 Similarly for ACCLAIM, 1+3+3+12+1+9+13+7=49 Hence option B is the correct answer

questions.question: If 19 July 2012 is Thursday, then what will be the day of the weekon 11 January 2018?
questions.explanation: Year Period Calculation Odd Days 2012 19 July To 31 Dec 12+31+30+31+30+31=165 165/7=4 2013 To 2017 4 Full Normal + 1 Leap Year Normal Year=365 Days Leap Year=366 Days 4+2=6 2018 1 Jan To 11 Jan 11 Days 4 Total Odd Days 0 Hence option A is correct answer.

questions.question: 40 is related to 53 following certain logic. Following the same logic, 8 is related to 13. To which of the following numbers is 68 related, following the same logic? [NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /deleting/multiplying etc., to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.]
Select the option that represents the letters that when sequentially placed from left to right in the blanks below will complete the letter series. q p _ r _ r q _ p _ r p _ q _ p r _ p _ q
questions.explanation: the correct order of the letters is qprrprq /qprrprq / qprrprq Hence option D will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark [?] in the given series? 30, 22, 36, 75, 178, ?
questions.explanation: 30× +7=22 22×1+14=36 36× +21=75 75×2+28=178 178× +35=480 Hence, option A will be the correct answer

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question marks[?] in the given series? 61, ?, 71, 73, 79,
questions.explanation: Series of prime numbers = 61,67,71,73,79, Hence, option B will be the correct answer

questions.question: Select the option that represents as per the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1. WAGGISH 2. WAX 3.WANE 4.WAVER 5.WARM
questions.explanation: The correct dictionary order is: 1. WAGGISH, 3. WANE, 5. WARM, 4. WAVER,2. WAX. Hence option D will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term andthe fourth term is related to the third term? EYES : FTFZ :: MIND : OENJ :: REST : ?
Select the pair that has the same analogy as the given pairs: 17 : 67 21 : 79
questions.explanation: 17 × 3+16= 67 21 × 3+16=79 Similarly, 34 × 3+16=118 Hence, option C will be the correct answer.

questions.question: The same operation[s] are followed in all the given number pairs except one. Find that odd number pair? [NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /deleting /multiplying etc., to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.]
questions.explanation: Sol: Option A –282:[28+3]2 Option B – 422 :[42+1]2 Option C – 342 :[34+1]2 Option D – 392 :[39+1]2 Hence, option A will be the correct answer

questions.question: Find the correct alternative on the basis of the given relationship. Kaziranga : Rhinoceros :: ?
questions.explanation: The logic followed here is: Logic: Animal reserves and the animals they are famous for. Here, Kaziranga : Rhinoceros → Kaziranga is famous for its population of Rhinoceros. Similarly, Periyar : Elephants → Periyar is famous for its population of Elephants Hence option B will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to sequentially replace the [_] signs and to balance the given equation. 75 _ 3 _ 17 _ 7 _ 1
questions.explanation: 75÷3 – 17 – 7 =25 – 24 = 1 Hence, option D will be the correct answer

questions.question: The position of how many letters will remain unchanged, if each of theletters in the word ‘DICTIONARY’ is arranged in alphabetical order.
questions.explanation: Position of two letters remains unchanged.

questions.question: Select the option in which the numbers do not share the same relationship as that shared by the given pair of numbers. [26, 38, 128] [42, 19, 122] [NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/deleting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.]
questions.explanation: [26+38] × 2 =128 [42+19]× 2 =122 Similar operation is being done in all options except B. [25+36]× 2 ≠ 183 Hence, option B will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged to make the following equation correct? 18 – 23 × 455 ÷ 7 + 28 = 377
questions.explanation: [Option C] 18 – 23 × 455 ÷ 7 + 28 = 377 After interchanging; 18 × 23 – 455÷7+28 = 414 – 65 + 28 =377 Hence, option C will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Three statements are given followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow/follows from the statements. Statements: Some cups are glasses. No glass is a stone. All stones are metals Conclusion: I. All cups can never be stones. II. No cup is a metal. III. All metals can never be glasses.
questions.explanation: Conclusion I  Conclusion II × Conclusion III

questions.question: In the figure, rectangle, square, circle, and triangle represent the regions of wheat, gram, maize, and rice cultivation, respectively In how many of the following areas more than one crops are cultivated?
questions.explanation: In the figure, rectangle, square, circle, and triangle represent the regions of wheat, gram, maize, and rice cultivation, respectively. There are 7 areas in which more than one crops has been cultivated. Hence,option D will be the correct answer.

questions.question: Find the odd letters from the given alternatives?
questions.explanation: B=2, C=3 and 2×3=6{F} G=7, H=8 and 7×8=56, 5+6=11{K} D=4, I=9 and 4×9=36, 3+6=9{I} H=8, J=10 and 8×10=80, 8+0=8{H} not S Hence option D will be the correct answer

questions.question: Eight people are sitting in two parallel rows with 4 people each in such a way that there is equal distance between adjacent persons and they are facing each other. In row I – L, M, N and O are seated and all of them are facing south. In row II – P, Q, R and S are seated and all of them are facing north. N sits next to the person at the extreme right end of their row and is facing R. S sits at the extreme left end of their row. Q sits to the immediate right of R and is facing L. O sits at the extreme left end of their row. Who sits at the extreme right end of the row of people facing South?
Which of the following is NOT an option related to the non-cooperation movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi?
options: Signing the resolution at Lahore for Poorna Swaraj
questions.explanation: The resolution for Purna Swaraj [Complete Independence] was passed at the Lahore Session of Congress in 1929, whereas the Non-Cooperation Movement [1920-1922] aimed at resisting British rule through nonviolent means like boycotting foreign goods, promoting Khadi [Charkha], and picketing liquor shops.

questions.question: Kishtwar National Park belongs to which state?
questions.explanation: Kishtwar National Park is located in the Kishtwar district of Jammu & Kashmir. It is known for its diverse wildlife, including the snow leopard, Himalayan black bear, and musk deer, and features a rugged mountainous terrain with alpine forests. > किश्तवाड़ राष्ट्रीय उद्यान जम्मू और कश्मीर के किश्तवाड़ जिले में स्थित है। यह अपने विविध वन्य जीवन के लिए जाना जाता है, जिसमें हिम तेंदुआ, हिमालयी काला भालू और कस्तूरी मृग शामिल हैं, और इसमें अल्पाइन जंगलों के साथ एक ऊबड़ पहाड़ी इलाका है।

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of Indian Constitution under the fundamental duties is related to the value and preservation of the rich heritage of our composite culture?
questions.explanation: It is provided under Article 51A [f] of the Indian Constitution. This clause, part of the Fundamental Duties listed in Article 51A, mandates that every citizen should "value and preserve the rich heritage of our composite culture."

questions.question: Vijay Kichlu belongs to which gharana? > विजय किचलू किस घराने से सम्बंधित हैं?
questions.explanation: Vijay Kichlu belonged to the Agra Gharana. He received guidance from Ustad Latafat Hussain Khan of the Agra Gharana and followed its distinctive style, particularly excelling in alapchari in the traditional dhrupad style. > विजय किचलू आगरा घराने से थे। उन्होंने आगरा घराने के उस्ताद लताफत हुसैन खान से मार्ग दर्शन प्राप्त किया और इसकी विशिष्ट शैली का पालन किया, विशेष रूप से पारंपरिक ध्रुपद शैली में अलापचारी में उत्कृष्ट प्रदर्शन किया।

questions.question: How do the muscle cells help in movement? > पेशीय कोशिकाएँ किस प्रकार गति करने में सहायता करती हैं?
options: The lining of the vessels inside help in movement.>अंदर की वाहिकाओं की परत गति में मदद करती है।
The contraction and relaxation of these cells result in movement.>इन कोशिकाओं के संकुचन और विश्राम के परिणाम स्वरूप गति होती है।
The blood flow of the cells helps in movement. >कोशिकाओं का रक्त प्रवाह गति में मदद करता है।
The thickness of the cell layer helps in movement.>कोशिका परत की मोटाई गति में सहायता करती है।
questions.explanation: Muscle cells help in movement by contracting and relaxing, which allows the body to perform various actions such as walking, breathing, and circulating blood. > मांसपेशियाँ सिकुड़ कर और शिथिल होकर गति करने में मदद करती हैं, जिससे शरीर को चलने, सांस लेने और रक्त संचार जैसी विभिन्न गतिविधियाँ करने की अनुमति मिलती है।

questions.question: National income at constant prices means: > स्थिर कीमतों पर राष्ट्रीय आय का अर्थ है:
options: Income estimated for next year prices.>अगले वर्ष की कीमतों के लिए अनुमानित आय।
Income estimated at base year prices.>आधार वर्ष की कीमतों पर अनुमानित आय।
Income estimated at current year prices. >चालू वर्ष की कीमतों पर अनुमानित आय।
Income estimated at any year prices. >किसी भी वर्ष की कीमतों पर अनुमानित आय।
questions.explanation: National income at constant prices refers to the income calculated by adjusting for inflation, using the prices of a chosen base year. This helps in comparing economic performance over different years without the effect of price changes. >स्थिर कीमतों पर राष्ट्रीय आय से तात्पर्य किसी चुने हुए आधार वर्ष की कीमतों का उपयोग कर के मुद्रास्फीति को समायोजित कर के गणना की गई आय से है। इससे मूल्य परिवर्तन के प्रभाव के बिना विभिन्न वर्षों में आर्थिक प्रदर्शन की तुलना करने में मदद मिलती है।

questions.question: Which of the following city is not located on the Bank of River Ganga? > निम्नलिखित में से कौनसा शहर गंगा नदी के तट पर स्थित नहीं है?
questions.explanation: Lucknow is not located on the banks of the Ganga River; it is situated on the banks of the Gomti River. Bhagalpur – Located on the Ganga River [Bihar] Ghazipur – Located on the Ganga River [Uttar Pradesh] Varanasi – Located on the Ganga River [Uttar Pradesh] > लखनऊ गंगा नदी के तट पर स्थित नहीं है; यह गोमती नदी के तट पर स्थित है। भागलपुर – गंगा नदी पर स्थित [बिहार] ग़ाज़ीपुर – गंगा नदी पर स्थित [उत्तर प्रदेश] वाराणसी – गंगा नदी पर स्थित [उत्तर प्रदेश]

questions.question: Which king established the Mauryan Empire in 322/321 B.C.? > किस राजा ने 322/321 ईसा पूर्व में मौर्य साम्राज्य की स्थापना की थी?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya established the Maurya Empire in 322 BC, marking the beginning of one of ancient India's most significant empires. > चंद्रगुप्त मौर्य ने 322 ईसा पूर्व में मौर्य साम्राज्य की स्थापना की, जो प्राचीन भारत के सबसे महत्वपूर्ण साम्राज्यों में से एक की शुरुआत थी।

questions.question: Which ruler was known by the name Farid Khan before ascending the throne? > कौन सा शासक गद्दी पर बैठने से पहले फ़रीद खान के नाम से जाना जाता था?
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri was originally named Farid Khan. He defeated Humayun and established the Suri Empire in North India. His rule [1540–1545] was known for administrative reforms, including the introduction of Rupiya [silver coin] and the Grand Trunk Road. His policies later influenced the Mughal administration, especially under Akbar. > शेरशाह सूरी का मूल नाम फरीद खान था। उसने हुमायूँ को हराया और उत्तर भारत में सूरी साम्राज्य की स्थापना की। उनका शासन [1540-1545] प्रशासनिक सुधारों के लिए जाना जाता था, जिसमें रुपिया [चांदी का सिक्का] और ग्रैंड ट्रंक रोड की शुरुआत शामिल थी। उनकी नीतियों ने बाद में मुगल प्रशासन, विशेषकर अकबर के अधीन, को प्रभावित किया।

questions.question: On immersing an iron nail in CuSO4 solution for a few minutes, you will observe? >लोहे की कील को CuSO4 विलयन में कुछ मिनट तक डुबाने पर आप देखेंगे?
options: No reaction takes place>कोई प्रतिक्रिया नहीं होती
The colour of the solution fades away>घोल का रंग उड़ जाता है
The surface of iron nails acquires a black coating >लोहे की कीलों की सतह पर काली परत चढ़ जाती है
The colour of the solution changes to green >घोल का रंग बदलकर हरा हो जाता है
questions.explanation: When an iron nail is immersed in copper sulfate [CuSO₄] solution, a displacement reaction occurs because iron is more reactive than copper. Iron displaces copper, forming iron sulfate [FeSO₄], which is green in color, causing the solution to turn green. > जब लोहे की कील को कॉपरसल्फेट [CuSO₄] के घोल में डुबोया जाता है, तो विस्थापन प्रतिक्रिया होती है क्योंकि लोहा तांबे की तुलना में अधिक प्रतिक्रियाशील होता है। लोहा तांबे को विस्थापित कर देता है, जिससे आयरनसल्फेट [FeSO₄] बनता है, जिसका रंग हरा होता है, जिससे घोल हरा हो जाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following part of plant provides mechanical strength & flexibility to the plants? > पौधे का निम्नलिखित में से कौनसा भाग पौधों को यांत्रिक शक्ति और लचीलापन प्रदान करता है?
questions.explanation: Collenchyma cells have unevenly thickened cell walls that provide mechanical support while still allowing flexibility, making them ideal for supporting growing parts of the plant. They are particularly important in regions like young stems and leaves, where both strength and the ability to stretch are required. > कोलेनकाइमा कोशिकाओं में असमान रूप से मोटी कोशिका दीवारें होती हैं जो लचीलेपन की अनुमति देते हुए यांत्रिक सहायता प्रदान करती हैं, जो उन्हें पौधे के बढ़ते हिस्सों को सहारा देने के लिए आदर्श बनाती हैं। वे युवा तनों और पत्तियों जैसे क्षेत्रों में विशेष रूप से महत्वपूर्ण हैं, जहां ताकत और खिंचाव की क्षमता दोनों की आवश्यकता होती है।

questions.question: Which state clinched their fifth Vijay Hazare Trophy 2024-25 title by defeating Vidarbha? > किस राज्य ने विदर्भ को हराकर अपना पांचवां विजय हजारे ट्रॉफी 2024-25 का खिताब जीता?
questions.explanation: Karnataka clinched their fifth Vijay Hazare Trophy title in the 2024–25 season by defeating Vidarbha in the final. The match took place on January 18, 2025, in Vadodara, where Karnataka posted a total of 348/6 and restricted Vidarbha to 312, securing a 36-run victory. The Vijay Hazare Trophy is an annual List A cricket tournament in India, and the 2024–25 edition was its 32nd iteration, held from December 21, 2024, to January 18, 2025. >कर्नाटक ने फाइनल में विदर्भ को हराकर 2024-25 सीज़न में अपना पांचवां विजय हजारे ट्रॉफी खिताब जीता। यह मैच 18 जनवरी, 2025 को वडोदरा में हुआ, जहां कर्नाटक ने कुल 348/6 का स्कोर बनाया और विदर्भ को 312 पर रोककर 36 रन से जीत हासिल की। विजय हजारे ट्रॉफी भारत में एक वार्षिक लिस्ट ए क्रिकेट टूर्नामेंट है, और 2024-25 संस्करण इसकी 32वीं पुनरावृत्तिथी, जो 21 दिसंबर, 2024 से 18 जनवरी, 2025 तक आयोजित की गई थी।

questions.question: Pravasi Bharatiya Divas is celebrated on which day? > प्रवासी भारतीय दिवस किस दिन मनाया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Pravasi Bharatiya Divas ([PBD] is celebrated on 9th January every year to recognize the contributions of the Indian diaspora. This date marks the return of Mahatma Gandhi from South Africa to India in 1915, which played a significant role in India's freedom struggle. > भारतीय प्रवासियों के योगदान को मान्यता देने के लिए हर साल 9 जनवरी को प्रवासी भारतीय दिवस [पीबीडी] मनाया जाता है। यह तिथि 1915 में महात्मा गांधी की दक्षिण अफ्रीका से भारत वापसी का प्रतीक है, जिन्होंने भारत के स्वतंत्रता संग्राम में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाई थी।

questions.question: Riccia, Moss [funaria], Marchantia are the examples of which group? > रिकिया, मॉस फनेरिया, मर्चेंटिया किस समूह के उदाहरण हैं?
questions.explanation: Funaria and Marchantia are examples of the group Bryophyta. Bryophytes are non-vascular plants that thrive in moist and shady environments. They are often referred to as the "amphibians" of the plant kingdom because they require water for reproduction. Funaria, commonly known as cord moss, and Marchantia, a type of liverwort, both exhibit these characteristics. > फनारिया और मर्चेंटिया ब्रायोफाइटा समूह के उदाहरण हैं। ब्रायोफाइट्स गैर-संवहनी पौधे हैं जो नम और छायादार वातावरण में पनपते हैं। उन्हेंअक्सर पौधे साम्राज्य के "उभयचर" के रूप में जाना जाता है क्योंकि उन्हें प्रजनन के लिए पानी की आवश्यकता होती है। फनारिया, जिसे आमतौर पर कॉर्ड मॉस के रूप में जाना जाता है, और मार्केंटिया, एक प्रकार का लिवरवॉर्ट, दोनों इन विशेषताओं को प्रदर्शित करते हैं।

questions.question: The UNESCO World Heritage Site Mahabodhi Vihar is situated in which state? > यूनेस्कोविश्वधरोहरस्थलमहाबोधिविहारकिसराज्यमेंस्थितहै?
questions.explanation: Mahabodhi Vihar [Mahabodhi Temple] is located in Bodh Gaya, Bihar. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and marks the place where Gautama Buddha attained enlightenment under the Bodhi tree. > महाबोधि विहार [महाबोधि मंदिर] बिहार के बोध गया में स्थित है। यह एक यूनेस्को विश्व धरोहर स्थल है और उस स्थान को चिह्नित करता है जहां गौतम बुद्ध को बोधि वृक्ष के नीचे ज्ञान प्राप्त हुआ था।

questions.question: Under the discretionary power of which Article of the Indian Constitution did the SC of India recognise sex work as a profession? > भारतीय संविधान के किस अनुच्छेद की विवेकाधीन शक्ति के तहत भारत के सर्वोच्च न्यायालय ने यौन कार्य को एक पेशे के रूप में मान्यता दी?
questions.explanation: Under Article 142 of the Indian Constitution, the Supreme Court has discretionary power to pass any order necessary for doing complete justice in any case pending before it. Using this power, the Supreme Court recognized sex work as a profession >भारतीय संविधान के अनुच्छेद 142 के तहत, सर्वोच्च न्यायालय के पास उसके समक्ष लंबित किसी भी मामले में पूर्ण न्याय करने के लिए आवश्यक कोई भी आदेश पारित करने की विवेकाधीन शक्ति है। इसी शक्ति का प्रयोग करते हुए सुप्रीम कोर्ट ने सेक्स वर्क को एक पेशे के रूप में मान्यता दी।

questions.question: Which among the following is a warm ocean current?
questions.explanation: The Kuroshio Current is a warm ocean current that flows northward along the eastern coast of Japan. It is part of the North Pacific Ocean circulation and significantly influences Japan's climate. Labrador Current – Cold current [North Atlantic] Falkland Current – Cold current [South Atlantic] Oyashio Current – Cold current [North Pacific] > कुरोशियो धारा एक गर्म समुद्री धारा है जो जापान के पूर्वी तट के साथ उत्तर की ओर बहती है। यह उत्तरी प्रशांत महासागर परिसंचरण का हिस्सा है और जापान की जलवायु को महत्वपूर्ण रूप से प्रभावित करता है। लैब्राडोर धारा – ठंडी धारा [उत्तरी अटलांटिक] फ़ॉकलैंड धारा – ठंडी धारा [दक्षिण अटलांटिक] ओयाशियो धारा – ठंडी धारा [उत्तरी प्रशांत]

questions.question: The excess of total expenditure of government Over its receipts Excluding borrowings is known as ? > सरकार के कुल व्यय का उसकी प्राप्तियों पर उधार को छोड़कर आधिक्य को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Fiscal Deficit refers to the excess of total government expenditure over its total revenue [excluding borrowings]. It indicates the shortfall that the government needs to meet through borrowing. > राजकोषीय घाटा का तात्पर्य उसके कुल राजस्व [उधार को छोड़कर] पर कुल सरकारी व्यय की अधिकता से है। यह उस कमी को इंगित करता है जिसे सरकार को उधार के माध्यम से पूरा करने की आवश्यकता है।

questions.question: The most abundant metal in the earth’s crust is? > पृथ्वी की पपड़ी में सबसे प्रचुर मात्रा में पाई जाने वाली धातु है?
questions.explanation: Aluminium [Al] is the most abundant metal in the Earth's crust, making up about 8.1% by weight. It is commonly found in minerals like bauxite [Al₂O₃xH₂O] and is widely used in industries due to its lightweight and corrosion resistance. > एल्यूमीनियम [Al] पृथ्वी की पपड़ी में सबसे प्रचुर धातु है, जिसका वजन लगभग 8.1% है। यह आमतौर पर बॉक्साइट [Al₂O₃ x H₂O] जैसे खनिजों में पाया जाता है और इसके हल्के वजन और संक्षारण प्रतिरोध के कारण उद्योगों में व्यापक रूप से उपयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Who is credited with creating the world famous Raaga Miya ki Malhar?
questions.explanation: Tansen, one of the Navratnas in Emperor Akbar's court, is credited with creating the world-famous Raag Miyan Ki Malhar. This raga is a variation of Raag Malhar and is believed to have the power to bring rain when sung with perfection. > सम्राट अकबर के दरबार के नवरत्नों में से एक तानसेन को विश्व प्रसिद्ध राग मियां की मल्हार बनाने का श्रेय दिया जाता है। यह राग राग मल्हार का ही एक रूप है और माना जाता है कि जब इसे पूर्णता के साथ गाया जाता है तो इसमें बारिश लाने की शक्ति होती है।

questions.question: The unit of luminous intensity is _______.? > ज्योति तीव्रता की इकाई _______ है।
questions.explanation: Candela [cd] is the SI unit of luminous intensity, which measures the brightness of a light source in a particular direction. > कैंडेला [cd] चमकदार तीव्रता की एसआई इकाई है, जो एक विशेष दिशा में प्रकाश स्रोत की चमक को मापती है।

questions.question: Which among the following is related to paika rebellion?
options: Manju Shah, & Devi Chaudharani>मंजु शाह और देवी चौधरानी
questions.explanation: The Paika Rebellion [1817] was led by Bakshi Jagabandhu in Odisha against British rule. The Paikas, the traditional warrior class, revolted due to unfair land revenue policies and British interference in their rights. This rebellion is often considered one of the first armed uprisings against British rule in India. > पाइका विद्रोह [1817] का नेतृत्व ब्रिटिश शासन के खिलाफ ओडिशा में बख्शी जगबंधु ने किया था। पाइका, पारंपरिक योद्धा वर्ग, ने अनुचित भूमि राजस्व नीतियों और उनके अधिकारों में ब्रिटिश हस्तक्षेप के कारण विद्रोह कर दिया। इस विद्रोह को अक्सर भारत में ब्रिटिश शासन के खिलाफ पहले सशस्त्र विद्रोहों में से एक माना जाता है।

questions.question: Which is the coldest layer of atmosphere? > वायुमंडल की सबसे ठंडी परत कौनसी है?
questions.explanation: The Mesosphere is the coldest layer of the atmosphere, with temperatures dropping as low as -90°C [-130°F]. It lies above the Stratosphere and below the Thermosphere and is where most meteors burn up upon entry into Earth's atmosphere. > मध्यमण्डल वायुमंडल की सबसे ठंडी परत है, जहां तापमान -90°C [-130°F] तक गिर जाता है। यह समतापमण्डल के ऊपर और तापमंडल के नीचे स्थित है और यहीं पर अधिकांश उल्काएं पृथ्वी के वायुमंडल में प्रवेश करते समय जल जाती हैं।

questions.question: Who has been appointed as Chairman of Central Board of Secondary Education [CBSE]?
questions.explanation: As of February 2025, the Chairman of the Central Board of Secondary Education [CBSE] is Rahul Singh, a 1996-batch Indian Administrative Service [IAS] officer of the Bihar cadre. He was appointed to this position in March 2024, succeeding Nidhi Chhibber. > फरवरी 2025 तक, केंद्रीय माध्यमिक शिक्षा बोर्ड [सीबीएसई] के अध्यक्ष राहुल सिंह हैं, जो बिहार कैडर के 1996-बैच के भारतीय प्रशासनिक सेवा [आईएएस] अधिकारी हैं। उन्हें निधि छिब्बर के स्थान पर मार्च 2024 में इस पद पर नियुक्त किया गया था।

questions.question: In India, the salaries of the Supreme Court judges are determined by?
options: Prime Minister of India >भारत के प्रधानमंत्री
Chief Justice of India>भारत के मुख्य न्यायाधीश
questions.explanation: In India, the salaries of Supreme Court judges are determined by the Parliament of India under Article 125 of the Constitution. The salaries and allowances can be revised through legislation passed by Parliament. > भारत में, सर्वोच्च न्यायालय के न्यायाधीशों का वेतन संविधान के अनुच्छेद 125 के तहत भारत की संसद द्वारा निर्धारित किया जाता है। वेतन और भत्ते को संसद द्वारा पारित कानून के माध्यम से संशोधित किया जा सकता है।

questions.question: From the salary of a Charted accountant, if 15% is used as house rent, 20% of the remaining is spent on household items, 25% of the remaining as income tax, and 10% of the remaining on clothing, then he is left with ₹13770. Find his total salary?
questions.explanation: Let the total salary be x. After house rent: 85% of x = 0.85x After output items: 80% of 0.85x = 0.68x After income tax: 75% of 0.68x = 0.51x After clothing: 90% of 0.51x = 0.459x Given: 0.459x = 13770 = = ₹30000 Total salary = ₹30000

questions.question: Simplify the following expression. [{70 − 60÷ 3 of 5} + {40− 10}÷ 10] + 600÷ 25 of {2 ×2}× {5 of 3} + 2 of 5.

Two trains of lengths 150 m and 250 m run on parallel lines. When they run in the same direction, they take 40 seconds to cross each other and, when they run in the opposite direction, they take 10 seconds to cross each other. What is the speed of the two trains in km/h?
questions.explanation: Lengths of trains = 150 m and 250 m Time in same direction = 40 sec → [150 + 250] ÷ 40 = 10 m/s → X - Y = 10 Time in opposite direction = 10 sec → [150 + 250] ÷ 10 = 40 m/s → X + Y = 40 Solving: [X + Y] + [X – Y] = 40 + 10 → 2X = 50 → X = 25 m/s →90 km/h X - Y = 10 →25 - Y = 10 → Y = 15 m/s →54 km/h Speed of first train = 25 m/s or 90 km/h Speed of second train = 15 m/s or 54 km/h

questions.question: A money bag contains one rupee, 5 rupee and 10 rupee coins in the ratio of 3 : 5 : 7 respectively. If the total amount in the bag is ₹1960,then find the number of coins of ₹10.
questions.explanation: Let the number of ₹1, ₹5, and ₹10 coins be 3x, 5x, and 7x respectively Forming the equation: [1 × 3x] + [5 × 5x] + [10 × 7x] = 1960 3x + 25x + 70x = 1960 98x = 1960 x = 20 Number of ₹10 coins: 7x = 7 × 20= 140

questions.question: A jar contains 35% syrup in the mixture of syrup and water. A part of this mixture is replaced by another mixture containing 22% syrup. Now, in the new mixture, the percentage of syrup of found to be 26%. What is the ratio in which these mixtures were mixed to obtain the new mixture?
questions.explanation: Using the method of alligation 35 22 26 4 9 Ratio would be 4:9

questions.question: What is the value of the following expression?
A dishonest merchant sells his grocery using weights that are 18% less than the true weights and sells at 64% higher price on his cost price. Find his total gain percentage.
questions.explanation: Let the true weight be 100 kg, but the merchant uses 82 kg [18% less]. Let the cost price of 100 kg be ₹100, so the actual CP = ₹82. Since, he makes 64% profit on his CP, SP of 82 kg = ₹164. Total gain % = × 100 = × 100 = 100%. Alternative: CP : SP 82:100 100:164 ----------- 1:2 × 100 = 100%.

questions.question: In a race of 150 m, R beats S by 10 m, If S takes a head start of 15 m, how long [in m] should the race be to have a tie between them?
questions.explanation: Distance 150 :140 Speed=15:14 In the same time, ratio of speed = ratio of distance Hence, ratio of distance travelled = 15 : 14 Difference in speed is 1 unit = 15m Length of full Race = 15 unit = 15×15 = 225 m

questions.question: Simple interest and compound interest on a principal at a certain rate of interest for 2 years is Rs 10000 and Rs 10400 respectively. What is the principal [in ₹]?
Study the Bar graph carefully and answer the question given below: What is the ratio of the people watching horror and action to the people watching animation and drama?
questions.explanation: Horror an action=83+36=119 Animation and drama=72+65=137 Required ratio =119:137

questions.question: The following table shows the percentage population of four states below poverty line and the proportion of male and female. If the total population of State D is 30,000 Then what is the number of Female above poverty line in state D?
questions.explanation: Total population = 30,000 Below Poverty Line [BPL] = 28% of 30,000 = 8,400 Above Poverty Line [APL] = 30,000 - 8,400 = 21,600 Female APL = [5/6] × 21,600 = 18,000.

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks his goods k% more than their cost price. If the shopkeeper allows a 25% discount and still gains 20%, then find the value of k?

A thief is noticed by a policeman from a distance of 800 m. The thief starts running and the policeman chases him. The thief and the policeman run at the rate of 10 km/h and 12 km/h, respectively. What is the distance [in metres] between them after 15 minutes?
questions.explanation: Relative speed of Police and thief is 2 km/h Distance covered by thief and police in 15 minutes is 2× ×15×60=500m So distance between them after 15 minutes would be 800- 500 300m

questions.question: If three spheres of radius 50cm, 40 cm and 30 cm are melted into one big sphere, then the radius of the big sphere is:
questions.explanation: Using the volume conservation formula: r13 + r23 + r33 = R3 503 + 403 + 303 = R3 Taking the cube root: R = = 60 So, the radius of the big sphere is 60cm

questions.question: Diagonals of a trapezium ABCD with AB∥DC, intersect each other at the point ‘O’. If AB = 7.5CD, find the ratio of the area of triangle COD to the area of triangle AOB.
questions.explanation: AB = 7.5CD = ratio of the area of triangle COD to the area of triangle AOB = { }2 = =

questions.question: To finish a work in 12 days, 10 workers are engaged. After 5 days, due to some urgency, the work is to be finished in next 2 days. How many more workers are to be engaged now to finish it on time?
questions.explanation: Total work = 10 workers × 12 days = 120 Work done in 5 days = 10 workers × 5 days = 50 Remaining work = 120 - 50 = 70 Remaining time = 2 days Required workers = 35 workers Additional workers needed = 35 - 10 = 25 workers

questions.question: A, B and C started a business in partnership. Initially, A invested Rs. 28,000, while B and C each invested Rs. 25,000 . After 4 months, A withdrew Rs. 3,000. After 2 more months, C invested Rs.12,000 more. Find the share of C [in Rs.] in the profit of Rs. 32800 at the end of the year?
questions.explanation: A’s investment: First 4 months: ₹28,000 × 4 = 1,12,000 After 4 months, A withdraws ₹3,000, so new investment = ₹25,000 Next 8 months: ₹25,000 × 8 = 2,00,000 Total for A = 1,12,000 + 2,00,000 = 3,12,000 B’s investment: Entire year: ₹25,000 × 12 = 3,00,000 C’s investment: First 6 months: ₹25,000 × 6 = 1,50,000 After 6 months, C invests ₹12,000 more, so new investment = ₹37,000 Next 6 months: ₹37,000 × 6 = 2,22,000 Total for C = 1,50,000 + 2,22,000 = 3,72,000 Total Investment Units Total = 3,12,000 + 3,00,000 + 3,72,000 = 9,84,000 C’s Share of Profit = × 32,800 = ₹12,400

questions.question: In an election, a voter has choices to vote for one candidate out of three candidates. There is also an option 'None of the Above' [NOTA] to reject all candidates. The three candidates get votes that are 7 times NOTA. The winner gets votes that are thrice the second runner-up's votes. The first runner up gets 900 votes more than NOTA and defeats the second runner up by 250 votes. What is the total number of votes polled?
questions.explanation: ∴ 5x + 3500 = 7x ∴ x = 1750 ∴ Total no of votes = 8x = 8×1750 = 14000

questions.question: The angle of elevation of a flying drone from a point on the ground is ????????°. After flying for ???? seconds the angle of elevation drops to ????????°. If the drone is flying horizontally at a constant height of ????????????????√???? m, the distance travelled by the drone is?
questions.explanation: From the right-angled triangle, tanθ = height / base For the initial position: tan 60° = x₁ = 1000 For the final position: tan 30° = x₂ = 3000 the distance travelled by the drone = x₂ - x₁ = 3000 - 1000 = 2000m So, the drone travelled 2000 meters.

questions.question: If a 9-digit number 937X728Y6 is divisible by 72, then one of the possible values of X + Y is
questions.explanation: For a number divisible by 72 it must be divisible by 8 and 9 For the divisibility of 8 check the last three digits 8Y6 The value of Y can be 1, 5 or 9 Now check the divisibility of 9 [Sum of all numbers must be divisible by 9] 6+X+Y Must be divisible by 9 Case one: if we take the value of Y as 1 then the value of X would be 2 X+Y=2 Not available in option Case 2: If we take the value of Y as 5 Then X would be 7 5+7=12 Satisfied Case3: If we take the value of Y as 9 Then X would be 3 9+3=12 Satisfied

questions.question: The area of the rhombus [in cm] having each side equal to 25 cm and one of its diagonals equal to 48 cm is?
questions.explanation: {24}2+{diagonal/2}2 =side2 576+{diagonal/2}2 =625 {diagonal/2}2=49 {diagonal/2} =7 Diagonal=14cm Area of rhombus = =336cm2

questions.question: In right-angled triangle ABC, C = 90°, CM is perpendicular on AB. If AB = 14 cm and BM = 6 cm, then find the length of CM.
questions.explanation: In a right-angle triangle the altitude to the hypotenuse follows CM2=AM×BM CM2=8×6 CM=√48 CM=4√3 cm

questions.question: Find the remainder, when we divide 2x3 – 3x2 + 6x – 4 by 2x – 3.
questions.explanation: 2x3 – 3x2 + 6x – 4 divide by 2x – 3 x = Substitute the value of x in the equation 2× - 3× + 6 - 4 9-4=5

questions.question: If a2+ = 223,then, find the value of a3+ .
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: No error. There is actually no grammatical error in any of the segments of the sentence. The sentence is correctly structured. The subject "Half of the class" is singular, so "has" is used correctly in segment B, and the sentence overall follows proper subject-verb agreement and word order.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Lata Mangeshkar was_________ with a natural talent for music.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Endowed." Explanation: The word "endowed" means to be naturally provided with a quality or talent, which fits the context of Lata Mangeshkar being naturally gifted with a talent for music. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Given – दिया गया – To be provided with something. Example: "She was given a gift for her birthday." • Found – पाया गया – Discovered or located. Example: "The treasure was found buried in the ground." • Endowed – प्रदान किया गया – To be naturally provided with a particular talent, quality, or gift. Example: "She was endowed with an incredible voice." • Entrusted – सौंपा गया – To be given responsibility for something. Example: "He was entrusted with the company's financial records." Conclusion: The best choice is "Endowed," as it correctly conveys that Lata Mangeshkar was naturally gifted with a talent for music.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. I hope you don’t mind me listening to music while you sleep.
options: me to listen to music while you sleep.
my listening to music while you sleep.
mine listening to music while you sleep.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: my listening to music while you sleep. Explanation: The original sentence "I hope you don’t mind me listening to music while you sleep." is grammatically incorrect because "me listening" should be changed to "my listening." Here's why: • The possessive pronoun "my" is required before a gerund [listening] to indicate that the action is associated with the speaker. • The use of "me" in this context is incorrect because "me" is an object pronoun, and the sentence needs a possessive pronoun to modify the gerund. Thus, the corrected sentence is: "I hope you don’t mind my listening to music while you sleep."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: Neither the employees nor the supervisor were taking their responsibility seriously.
Neither the employees nor the supervisor was taking their responsibility seriously.
Neither the employees nor the supervisor were taking his responsibility seriously.
Neither the employees nor the supervisor was taking his responsibility seriously.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Neither the employees nor the supervisor was taking his responsibility seriously. Explanation: • The phrase "Neither the employees nor the supervisor" is a compound subject. When two subjects are connected by "neither...nor," the verb agrees with the noun closest to it. In this case, "supervisor" is singular, so the verb should also be singular, i.e., "was." • Additionally, "his responsibility" is used to refer to the supervisor's singular responsibility, which is correct. Therefore, the correct sentence is: "Neither the employees nor the supervisor was taking his responsibility seriously."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: "Elementary is the correct spelling. Explanation: • Elementary – मूलभूत – Relating to the basic or fundamental principles of a subject. Example: "The teacher focused on teaching elementary math concepts to the students." Incorrect Spellings and Their Corrections: • Begining [Incorrect] – Beginning [Correct] – शुरुआत – The start of something. Example: "The beginning of the story was very exciting." • Coalscence [Incorrect] – Coalescence [Correct] – संलयन – The process of things coming together to form one whole. Example: "The coalescence of different ideas led to an innovative solution." • Compitition [Incorrect] – Competition [Correct] – प्रतिस्पर्धा – A contest or event where people or teams compete against each other. Example: "She won the competition for best science project."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The team is scheduled to carry off the project next week.
questions.explanation: Use "Carry out." Explanation: The phrase "carry out" means to perform or complete a task, project, or plan, which fits the context of the sentence where the team is scheduled to complete the project next week. • The sentence conveys that the team is scheduled to perform or finish the project next week. Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Carry out – कार्यान्वित करना / पूरा करना – To perform or complete a task, project, or plan. Example: "The team is scheduled to carry out the project next week." • Carry away – ले जाना / भावनाओं में बह जाना – To take something away physically, or to be emotionally overwhelmed. Example: "She was carried away by the excitement of the event." • Carry off – सफलतापूर्वक करना / काबू पाना – To succeed in doing something difficult or handle something with skill and confidence. Example: "He carried off the performance flawlessly, impressing everyone in the audience." • Carry through – पूरा करना / सफल बनाना – To complete something, especially when there are challenges or obstacles. Example: "He managed to carry through his plan despite the difficulties." Correct Sentence: "The team is scheduled to carry out the project next week."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - You should take after your sister who is a DILIGENT student.
questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Answer: Hardworking Explanation: The word "diligent" means showing careful and persistent work or effort, which makes "hardworking" the correct synonym. The sentence suggests that the person is a dedicated and consistent student, which aligns with the meaning of "hardworking." Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Diligent – मेहनती – Showing careful and persistent effort or work. Example: "She is a diligent student who always finishes her assignments on time." • Intelligent– बुद्धिमान – Having the ability to acquire and apply knowledge and skills. Example: "He is an intelligent student, but not necessarily diligent in his studies." • Eminent– प्रमुख – Famous or respected in a particular field. Example: "The eminent scientist gave a lecture at the conference." • Promising – आशाजनक – Likely to succeed or develop well in the future. Example: "The promising student impressed the teachers with her skills." Conclusion: The correct synonym for "diligent" is "hardworking", as both words refer to putting in consistent and persistent effort to accomplish something.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. VIRTUE
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Vice Explanation: The word "virtue" refers to moral excellence, righteousness, or good qualities. The opposite of this would be "vice," which refers to immoral or wicked behavior. Let's see the meaning of all the options along with their usage: • Virtue – सद्गुण – Moral excellence or good qualities. Example: "Honesty is considered a virtue in all cultures." • Vice – दोष / पाप – Immoral or wicked behaviour, bad habits. Example: "He was known for his vices, including gambling and dishonesty." • Failure – विफलता – Lack of success or not achieving a goal. Example: "He faced failure after failure in his attempts to start the business." • Fault – दोष – A flaw or defect in something, or responsibility for a mistake. Example: "The fault for the accident was found to lie with the driver." • Offence – अपराध / अवज्ञा – An illegal act or wrongdoing. Example: "The police arrested him for committing an offence." Conclusion: The correct antonym for "virtue" is "vice", as "vice" refers to immoral behaviour or bad habits, which directly contrasts with "virtue" meaning moral excellence or good qualities.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. All of them are surprised_______ her rudeness.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: At Explanation: The correct preposition to use with "surprised" when referring to someone's behavior or actions is "at." We use "at" to indicate the cause or reason for being surprised, especially when referring to a specific event or behavior. Corrected Sentence: "All of them are surprised at her rudeness."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. A white elephant
options: An extinct species of elephant found in Burma
A report by the government to give information
Huge colossal waste of human energy
Costly and troublesome possession useless to its owner
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Costly and troublesome possession useless to its owner. Explanation: The idiom "A white elephant" refers to something that is costly and troublesome but ultimately useless or of little value to its owner. It usually describes a possession that requires a lot of resources, time, and effort to maintain but does not provide much in return. Origin of the Idiom: The term originated from the ancient practice in Southeast Asia, where a white elephant was considered sacred and highly valuable. However, owning one was seen as a great burden, as it could not be used for labour, and its maintenance was costly. The phrase "white elephant" eventually came to represent any possession that is more of a burden than a benefit. Example: "His new sports car turned out to be a white elephant—constantly breaking down and costing him a fortune to repair." [उसकी नई स्पोर्ट्स कार एक सफेद हाथी बन गई—बार-बार खराब हो रही थी और उसे ठीक करने में एक भाग खर्च हो रहा था।] Conclusion: The correct meaning of "A white elephant" is "Costly and troublesome possession useless to its owner," as it refers to an item or possession that is more trouble than it's worth.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: Error in segment: what was the reason CONCEPT: • When "what" is joining two clauses, it should be followed by a normal affirmative sentence. In this case, "what was the reason" is incorrect because it forms a question structure instead of an affirmative one. • The correct structure should be "what the reason was." Corrected Sentence: "He didn’t understand what the reason was she didn’t attend the meeting."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. ADORN
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Beautify. Explanation: The word "adorn" means to decorate or enhance the appearance of something, typically to make it more attractive or beautiful. The synonym that most closely matches this meaning is "beautify", which also refers to making something more beautiful or visually appealing. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Adorn – सजाना / अलंकृत करना – To decorate or enhance the appearance of something. Example: "She adorned the room with beautiful flowers and colorful paintings." • Beautify– सुंदर बनाना – To make something more beautiful or visually appealing. Example: "They beautified the garden with vibrant flowers and sculptures." • Trust – विश्वास करना – To believe in the reliability, truth, or ability of something. Example: "I trust my friend to keep my secret." • Write – लिखना – To form letters or words on a surface with a pen, pencil, or other means. Example: "She likes to write in her journal every evening." • Suspect– संदिग्ध होना – To have a belief that something may be true or happen, often without full evidence. Example: "He suspected that someone was lying about the incident." Conclusion: The correct synonym for "adorn" is "beautify", as both words involve making something more attractive or aesthetically pleasing.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- She is so FASTIDIOUS about her food that I never invite her for dinner.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Adjustable. Explanation: The word "fastidious" means being very attentive to detail, hard to please, or excessively concerned about cleanliness or other aspects, particularly in a demanding way. The antonym should describe a quality of being flexible or easy-going, which makes "adjustable" the most appropriate antonym. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Fastidious – अत्यधिक सावधान / बहुत चुस्त – Being very particular, difficult to please, or demanding attention to detail. Example: "She is so fastidious about her food that I never invite her for dinner." • Fussy– नखरे वाला / बहुत चुस्त – Similar in meaning to "fastidious," describing someone who is hard to please and overly concerned with small details. Example: "He’s too fussy about his clothes." • Cooperative – सहयोगी / मिलनसार – Willing to work together and be helpful, but doesn't directly oppose "fastidious." Example: "The team was cooperative in completing the project." • Focused – केंद्रित – Giving attention or concentration to something. Example: "She was focused on finishing her homework." • Adjustable – समायोज्य – Capable of being changed or made flexible, implying ease and adaptability. Example: "The chair is adjustable, so you can set it to your comfort." Conclusion: The correct antonym for "fastidious" is "adjustable", as it describes flexibility and ease, whereas "fastidious" refers to being difficult to please or overly particular.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The King's eldest son is the _______ to the throne.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "The King's eldest son is the Heir to the throne." This means that the King's eldest son is the person who will inherit the throne after the King. Explanation of all Options: • Heir – उत्तराधिकारी – A person who will inherit a title, property, or position. Example: "The prince is the heir to the throne." • Here– यहाँ – Used to indicate a location or presence. Example: "Come here and sit down." • Hair– बाल – A growth on the human head or body. Example: "She has long, curly hair." • Haer – This is not a meaningful word in English. Conclusion: The correct word is "Heir," as it correctly describes the person who will inherit the throne.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A thing likely to be easily broken
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Brittle. Here are the explanations for all options: • Brittle – नाजुक / टूटने योग्य – Easily broken or fragile, especially when exposed to pressure or stress. Example: "The old glass was brittle and shattered when it fell." "Brittle" directly refers to something that is likely to be easily broken. • Vulnerable – संवेदनशील / असुरक्षित – Susceptible to harm, but not necessarily easily broken physically. Example: "The village is vulnerable to flooding during heavy rains." "Vulnerable" suggests being at risk, but it doesn't specifically mean easily broken. • Flexible – लचीला – Capable of bending without breaking. Example: "The flexible material allowed the chair to bend without cracking." "Flexible" refers to something that can bend or change shape, not something easily broken. • Delicate – नाजुक / कोमल – Fragile or easily damaged, but not always in the same sense as easily broken. Example: "She handled the delicate porcelain vase carefully." "Delicate" refers to something fragile but not necessarily prone to breaking as "brittle" does. Conclusion: The correct word is "Brittle," as it specifically refers to something that is likely to break or shatter easily when subjected to force.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. My attempt to learn a new language have been going in fits and starts.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Irregularly. Explanation: The idiom "in fits and starts" means that something happens in an inconsistent or irregular manner, with periods of activity followed by times of inactivity. This phrase suggests that the process or progress is uneven or sporadic. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase "in fits and starts" likely originates from the idea of something starting and stopping abruptly, like a machine that begins and stops repeatedly. It has come to describe anything that happens in bursts, without a steady or continuous pattern. Example: "My attempts to learn a new language have been going in fits and starts, with some weeks where I study a lot, and others where I don't study at all." Conclusion: The meaning of "in fits and starts" is best described as occurring irregularly.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. The study of ancient civilizations
questions.explanation: "The study of ancient civilizations is called Archaeology." This means that archaeology is the field that focuses on studying ancient civilizations through their remains and artefacts. Explanation of the Other Options: • History – इतिहास – Refers to the study of past events and people but not specifically ancient civilizations' physical remains. Example: "History records significant events." • Anthropology – मानवशास्त्र – Studies humans, their cultures, and evolution, not just ancient civilizations. Example: "Anthropology studies human behavior." • Ethnology– जातिविज्ञान – Focuses on cultures and societies, not ancient physical remains. Example: "Ethnology compares cultural practices." • Archaeology– पुरातत्त्व – Studies ancient civilizations through artifacts and excavations. Example: "Archaeology uncovers ancient tools and structures." Conclusion: The correct word is "Archaeology," as it directly relates to studying ancient civilizations' physical remains.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. skills and concepts are harder in a world where soft skills are also hard skills B. performance management is often equated with teacher attendance, yet C. scores can be measured based on continuous assessments or end-of-year exams D. a teacher needs to be evaluated on outputs and inputs
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: BDCA Let's break down the logical flow and connection between each sentence: B: "performance management is often equated with teacher attendance, yet" This sentence introduces the topic of performance management in the context of teaching. It highlights a common misconception or oversimplification that performance management is merely about tracking teacher attendance. The word "yet" suggests there's more to performance management than just attendance. D: "a teacher needs to be evaluated on outputs and inputs" Following the introduction of the topic, this sentence provides a more comprehensive approach to evaluating teacher performance. It emphasizes that both the outputs and inputs should be considered. This sentence logically follows B because it offers a corrective perspective on how performance management should be conducted. C: "scores can be measured based on continuous assessments or end-of-year exams" This sentence discusses specific methods for evaluating student performance or teacher effectiveness. It fits well after D because it elaborates on how the "outputs" mentioned in D can be measured. Continuous assessments and end-of-year exams are common methods used to evaluate student learning outcomes, which are a key part of a teacher's outputs. A: "skills and concepts are harder in a world where soft skills are also hard skills" This sentence concludes the paragraph by highlighting the complexity of teaching and learning in the modern world. It suggests that both technical skills and soft skills are essential and challenging to master. This sentence can logically follow the discussion on evaluation methods, as it underscores the importance of considering all aspects of a teacher's role in the evaluation process. Conclusion - The order BDCA creates a logical flow from introducing the topic of performance management, to discussing a more comprehensive evaluation approach, elaborating on specific evaluation methods, and finally highlighting the complexity of teaching and learning. This order helps to build a coherent argument about the need for a nuanced approach to evaluating teacher performance.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. He teaches us grammar.
options: Grammar was taught to us by him.
We are taught grammar by him.
Grammar will be taught to us by him.
We were teached grammar by him.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The given sentence follows the active voice structure: Subject + Verb + Object → He teaches us grammar. • To convert it into passive voice, we use the structure: Object + am/are/is + past participle + by + Subject. Conclusion: Applying this rule, the passive form becomes: "We are taught grammar by him."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The teacher said, “Why can’t you come to school on time, Reema?”
options: The teacher asked Reema why she can’t come to school on time.
The teacher asked Reema why can’t she come to school on time.
The teacher asked Reema why couldn’t she come to school on time.
The teacher asked Reema why she couldn’t come to school on time.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The teacher asked Reema why she couldn’t come to school on time. Explanation: • The given sentence is in direct speech and follows the structure: The teacher said, “Why can’t you come to school on time, Reema?” • To convert it into indirect speech, follow these rules: • The reporting verb "said" changes to "asked" because the sentence is a question. • The question word "why" remains unchanged. • The direct speech sentence is in the present tense ["can’t"], and when converting to indirect speech, we change it to the past tense, which becomes "couldn’t" because the reporting verb is in the past tense. • The pronoun "you" changes to "she" to match Reema as the subject. Corrected Sentence: "The teacher asked Reema why she couldn’t come to school on time."

questions.question: This past decade has witnessed a ____1____ in wildlife tourism that has doubled and tripled the tourist load in several of the more accessible national parks and reserves. But serious infrastructural shortcomings have brought us ____2____ close to a breakdown of the ecosystem ‘s capacity to absorb this load. Not to speak of the ecosystem that is being battered by humans. A road area, which ____3____ four vehicles a day, ten years ago, is now being used by 12 a day. The road area wildlife population of that area has in any case gone up. A collision of interests is ____4____. The result is tourist traffic, which is generally quite ignorant of wildlife, ecology, and conservation values, who leave the refuge no wiser than when they came in leaving behind for good measure the ____5____ of their presence: plastic bags, cigarette packets, bottle, paper, a lot of which is nonbiodegradable and toxic. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Surge Explanation - The noun "surge" refers to a sudden, significant increase or rise in something, such as a surge in popularity, a surge in prices, or a surge in tourist numbers. In this context, "surge" accurately describes the rapid growth in wildlife tourism, which has doubled and tripled the tourist load in several national parks and reserves. Let's see why other options are wrong: • Consistency: This noun refers to the quality of being consistent or uniform, which doesn't fit the context of rapid growth. • Dip: This noun refers to a decrease or decline, which is the opposite of what's described in the passage. • Ban: This noun refers to an official prohibition or restriction, which doesn't relate to the growth of wildlife tourism.

questions.question: This past decade has witnessed a ____1____ in wildlife tourism that has doubled and tripled the tourist load in several of the more accessible national parks and reserves. But serious infrastructural shortcomings have brought us ____2____ close to a breakdown of the ecosystem ‘s capacity to absorb this load. Not to speak of the ecosystem that is being battered by humans. A road area, which ____3____ four vehicles a day, ten years ago, is now being used by 12 a day. The road area wildlife population of that area has in any case gone up. A collision of interests is ____4____. The result is tourist traffic, which is generally quite ignorant of wildlife, ecology, and conservation values, who leave the refuge no wiser than when they came in leaving behind for good measure the ____5____ of their presence: plastic bags, cigarette packets, bottle, paper, a lot of which is nonbiodegradable and toxic. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Precariously. Explanation - The adverb "precariously" means in a way that is uncertain, unstable, or risky. In this context, it suggests that the ecosystem is close to collapsing due to the increased tourist load. Let's see why other options are wrong: • Vivaciously: This adverb means in a lively or energetic manner, which doesn't fit the context of instability. • Courageously: This adverb means in a brave or courageous manner, which also doesn't fit the context. • Vigilantly: This adverb means in a watchful or attentive manner, which doesn't convey the sense of instability or risk.

questions.question: This past decade has witnessed a ____1____ in wildlife tourism that has doubled and tripled the tourist load in several of the more accessible national parks and reserves. But serious infrastructural shortcomings have brought us ____2____ close to a breakdown of the ecosystem ‘s capacity to absorb this load. Not to speak of the ecosystem that is being battered by humans. A road area, which ____3____ four vehicles a day, ten years ago, is now being used by 12 a day. The road area wildlife population of that area has in any case gone up. A collision of interests is ____4____. The result is tourist traffic, which is generally quite ignorant of wildlife, ecology, and conservation values, who leave the refuge no wiser than when they came in leaving behind for good measure the ____5____ of their presence: plastic bags, cigarette packets, bottle, paper, a lot of which is nonbiodegradable and toxic. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The verb "accommodated" is in the past tense, indicating that the road area previously handled a certain number of vehicles per day. In this context, "accommodated" correctly describes the past situation, contrasting with the current increased traffic.

questions.question: This past decade has witnessed a ____1____ in wildlife tourism that has doubled and tripled the tourist load in several of the more accessible national parks and reserves. But serious infrastructural shortcomings have brought us ____2____ close to a breakdown of the ecosystem ‘s capacity to absorb this load. Not to speak of the ecosystem that is being battered by humans. A road area, which ____3____ four vehicles a day, ten years ago, is now being used by 12 a day. The road area wildlife population of that area has in any case gone up. A collision of interests is ____4____. The result is tourist traffic, which is generally quite ignorant of wildlife, ecology, and conservation values, who leave the refuge no wiser than when they came in leaving behind for good measure the ____5____ of their presence: plastic bags, cigarette packets, bottle, paper, a lot of which is nonbiodegradable and toxic. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Inevitable Explanation - The adjective "inevitable" means certain to happen or unavoidable. In this context, it suggests that a collision of interests between tourists and wildlife is unavoidable or certain to occur. Let's see why other options are wrong: • Irregular: This adjective means not following a regular pattern or rule, which doesn't fit the context. • Admittable: This adjective is not commonly used and doesn't fit the context. • Adjustable: This adjective means capable of being adjusted or modified, which doesn't convey the sense of certainty or unavoidability.

questions.question: This past decade has witnessed a ____1____ in wildlife tourism that has doubled and tripled the tourist load in several of the more accessible national parks and reserves. But serious infrastructural shortcomings have brought us ____2____ close to a breakdown of the ecosystem ‘s capacity to absorb this load. Not to speak of the ecosystem that is being battered by humans. A road area, which ____3____ four vehicles a day, ten years ago, is now being used by 12 a day. The road area wildlife population of that area has in any case gone up. A collision of interests is ____4____. The result is tourist traffic, which is generally quite ignorant of wildlife, ecology, and conservation values, who leave the refuge no wiser than when they came in leaving behind for good measure the ____5____ of their presence: plastic bags, cigarette packets, bottle, paper, a lot of which is nonbiodegradable and toxic. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Detritus. Explanation - The noun "detritus" refers to the remains or fragments of something that has been broken down or destroyed. In this context, it describes the waste and leftovers such as plastic bags, cigarette packets, and bottles that tourists leave behind. Let's see why other options are wrong: • Stature: This noun refers to a person's height or overall physical appearance, which doesn't fit the context. • Conjuncture: This noun refers to a combination of circumstances or a critical moment, which doesn't fit the context. • Covetous: This adjective means having a strong desire for something, especially something that belongs to someone else, which doesn't fit the context.
